text:
The highly anticipated second season of Percy Jackson and the Olympians has finally landed onDisney Plus.

Witha new heroic adventure into uncharted waters, the first two episodes of the latest instalmentarrived on the streaming platform today (December 10) but fans will have a wait on their hands as not every episode is released straight away with new installments being released every Wednesday.

Based on thesecond book in Rick Riordan's best selling series,The Sea Monsters, Disney Plus has promised "thrilling new monsters" as well as "higher stakes than ever" as the young demigods return in a bid to save Camp Half Blood and their friend Grover.

Disney teases: "After Camp Half-Blood’s protective border is breached, Percy Jackson embarks on an epic odyssey into the Sea of Monsters in search of his best friend Grover and the one thing that may save the camp — the legendary Golden Fleece.

"With help from Annabeth, Clarisse and his newfound cyclops half-brother Tyson, Percy’s survival becomes essential to stopping Luke, the Titan Kronos, and their impending plan to bring down Camp Half-Blood — and ultimately, Olympus."



Read more:Percy Jackson star is getting his diver’s license for season 2, Sea of Monsters

Read more:Percy Jackson and The Olympians was inspired by author's dyslexic son

Starring Walker Scobell as Percy Jackson, Leah Sava Jeffries as Annabeth, Aryan Simhadri as Grover and Charlie Bushnell as Luke, fans will also be treated to special guest stars including Lin-Manuel Miranda, Jason Mantzoukas, Glynn Turman and Timothy Simons.

Viewers may also recognise Virginia Kull, Courtney B. Vance, Andra Day, Adam Copeland, Sandra Bernhard, Margaret Cho, Kristen Schaal, Tamara Smart, Rosemarie DeWitt, Toby Stephens and more.

Earlier this year an official trailer dropped, promising more action packed adventures. In the intense clip, Percy Jackson can be heard saying: "You guys get nightmares, right?

"I usually get ones that tell me the future.... or try to kill me."

And fans were quick to share their enthusiasm as one person wrote: "GOOSEBUMPS", another said: " Looks Epic! They gotta get the other series filmed asap, though. Percy is nearly a fully grown adult."

A third added: " WE ARE SO READY!!" A fourth added: " Fantastic trailer! The sets and special effects look really great!

"We see several Olympian gods, in addition to Hermes and Dinosius, Ares and Poseidon will also be there! I can't wait to see this new season!"

But as season 2 lands, we have taken a look at what is next in the Percy Jackson universe.



We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Yes, Disney Plus has confirmed news on a third instalment, ahead of season 2's premier.

The streaming platform confirmed season 3 is currently in production in Vancouver as they added: " In the meantime, fans can relive the excitement of the first season, now streaming on Disney+."

Season 3 will likely follow the order of the books and so will be adapted from the third book on the best selling series called The Titan's Curse.

The Titan's Curse follows Percy on a quest in a bid to rescue Annabeth and goddess Artemis as they face an impending war as well as a dangerous beast.

A book synopsis teases: "It's not everyday you find yourself in combat with a half-lion, half-human. But when you're the son of a Greek god, it happens."

There has been no official release date shared yet for season 3, however given the fact it is in production could mean fans will likely have a wait on their hands yet. However, rumours suggest it could be released in December next year.

Percy Jackson and the Olympians season 2 is streaming on Disney Plus

title: Will there be a Percy Jackson and the Olympians Season 3? | TV & Radio | Showbiz & TV | Express.co.uk
author: Aaliyah Rugg
description: Percy Jackson and the Olympians season 2 is set to hit screens in a matter of days, with talks turning to a third instalment
